title: Algorithm to Compilation Co-design: An Integrated View of Neural Network Sparsity
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of balancing expanding AI capabilities with making them broadly impactful through sparse neural networks. It examines interactions between model regularization, compilation, and inference execution to determine end-to-end performance.
main_contributions: Implementation of both unstructured and structured sparsification of attention weights in BERT
Use of BSR sparsity optimizations in the TVM compiler
Analysis of interactions between model regularization, compilation, and inference execution
Examination of how algorithms and compiler optimizations interact at different abstraction levels
methods: Structured Sparsification: Following conventional pruning formulation with optimization problem minimize Σf(w) + λ||w||p
Unstructured Sparsification: Implementation of pruning techniques
BSR Sparsity Optimizations: Block Sparse Row format optimizations in TVM compiler

key_findings: Sparsity remains relevant in transformer models for computer vision and NLP
Current CPUs/GPUs don't immediately benefit from sparsity, but next-gen hardware has native support
Interactions between model regularization, compilation, and execution have critical effects on performance
